response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level background, but the role demands a senior hands-on C++ developer for industrial automation. His C++ exposure is limited to a short 7-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, with no evidence of STL-heavy modern C++, MQTT/IoT protocol work, IEC 62443 or SDL security practices, or industrial-domain experience. Total experience exceeds the range, but domain and core-stack alignment is weak.
strengths: Broad 15+ years of software engineering across web, embedded, and desktop systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, caching, micro-services at millions of req/s)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience (brief role ending 2015); recent work is TypeScript/Node/DLang
No industrial cybersecurity background (IEC 62443, SDL) or MQTT/OT protocol experience
No message-broker/event-driven C++ architecture or industrial automation domain exposure

key_factors.factor: Strong general seniority and systems performance experience
